gaps: No test case covers the scenario of an earbud remaining connected after being placed in the charging case with the lid closed.
No test case covers the primary/secondary earbud connection behavior when one earbud is placed back in the case while the other remains connected.
No test case covers the Free 60 earbuds pairing, connection, or charging case behavior at all.
All existing test cases are focused on app onboarding, EULA, and location permissions — none relate to earbud connection/disconnection behavior.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-001: Primary earbud disconnects when placed in charging case with lid closed
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Verify both earbuds show as 'Connected' in the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app.
3. Remove the primary (left) earbud from your ear.
4. Place the primary earbud into the charging case.
5. Close the charging case lid securely.
6. Wait 5 seconds.
7. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of the primary earbud.
generated_testcases.expected: The primary earbud should disconnect from the phone and no longer appear as 'Connected' in Bluetooth settings or the companion app. The secondary earbud may remain connected or also disconnect depending on the device's behavior.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-002: Secondary earbud disconnects when placed in charging case with lid closed
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Verify both earbuds show as 'Connected' in the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app.
3. Remove the secondary (right) earbud from your ear.
4. Place the secondary earbud into the charging case.
5. Close the charging case lid securely.
6. Wait 5 seconds.
7. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of the secondary earbud.
generated_testcases.expected: The secondary earbud should disconnect from the phone and no longer appear as 'Connected' in Bluetooth settings or the companion app. The primary earbud should remain connected if it is still in use.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-003: Both earbuds disconnect when both placed in charging case with lid closed
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Verify both earbuds show as 'Connected' in the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app.
3. Remove both earbuds from your ears.
4. Place both earbuds into the charging case.
5. Close the charging case lid securely.
6. Wait 5 seconds.
7. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of both earbuds.
generated_testcases.expected: Both earbuds should disconnect from the phone and no longer appear as 'Connected' in Bluetooth settings or the companion app. The device should show 'Disconnected' or 'Not Connected' for the earbuds.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-004: Primary earbud remains connected when placed in charging case with lid open
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Verify both earbuds show as 'Connected' in the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app.
3. Remove the primary (left) earbud from your ear.
4. Place the primary earbud into the charging case.
5. Keep the charging case lid OPEN.
6. Wait 5 seconds.
7. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of the primary earbud.
generated_testcases.expected: The primary earbud should remain connected to the phone while the lid is open, as the case lid closure is the trigger for disconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-005: Primary earbud reconnects after being removed from charging case with lid closed
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Place the primary earbud into the charging case and close the lid.
3. Wait 5 seconds and verify the primary earbud has disconnected.
4. Open the charging case lid.
5. Remove the primary earbud from the case.
6. Wait up to 10 seconds for reconnection.
7. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of the primary earbud.
generated_testcases.expected: The primary earbud should automatically reconnect to the phone within 10 seconds of being removed from the charging case. It should appear as 'Connected' in Bluetooth settings or the companion app.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-006: Primary earbud role transfer when primary is placed in case while secondary remains in use
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Verify the primary (left) earbud is marked as primary/active in the companion app.
3. While the secondary (right) earbud remains in your ear and playing audio, remove the primary earbud from your ear.
4. Place the primary earbud into the charging case and close the lid.
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for connection status.
7. Verify audio continues to play through the secondary earbud without interruption.
generated_testcases.expected: The secondary earbud should take over as the primary connected earbud. Audio should continue playing through the secondary earbud without interruption. The companion app should reflect the secondary earbud as the active/primary connection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-007: Earbuds do not reconnect when charging case lid is closed after brief opening
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Place both earbuds into the charging case and close the lid.
3. Wait 5 seconds and verify both earbuds have disconnected.
4. Open the charging case lid for 2 seconds, then close it again without removing any earbuds.
5. Wait 10 seconds.
6. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of both earbuds.
generated_testcases.expected: Both earbuds should remain disconnected. Opening and closing the lid without removing earbuds should not trigger a reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-008: Verify charging case LED behavior when earbud is placed inside and lid is closed
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Remove the primary earbud from your ear.
3. Place the primary earbud into the charging case.
4. Close the charging case lid.
5. Observe the LED indicator on the front of the charging case.
6. Wait 10 seconds and observe any LED changes.
generated_testcases.expected: The charging case LED should illuminate briefly (e.g., green, orange, or red depending on charge level) to indicate the earbud is seated and charging, then turn off after a few seconds to conserve battery.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-009: Verify audio routing when primary earbud is placed in case during a call
steps: 1. Ensure both earbuds are fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth.
2. Initiate a phone call and ensure audio is playing through both earbuds.
3. During the call, remove the primary earbud from your ear.
4. Place the primary earbud into the charging case and close the lid.
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Verify audio routing and call status.
generated_testcases.expected: The call should remain active. Audio should seamlessly route to the secondary earbud (if it remains in use) or to the phone's speaker/mic if both earbuds are placed in the case. The call should not drop.

generated_testcases.title: TC-EARBUD-CONN-010: Verify connection persistence when earbud is placed in case with low battery
steps: 1. Ensure the primary earbud has low battery (e.g., less than 10%).
2. Connect both earbuds to the phone via Bluetooth.
3. Remove the primary earbud from your ear.
4. Place the primary earbud into the charging case and close the lid.
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Check the Bluetooth settings and/or companion app for the connection status of the primary earbud.
generated_testcases.expected: The primary earbud should disconnect from the phone when placed in the charging case with the lid closed, regardless of battery level. Low battery should not prevent the disconnection behavior.
